title: Measurement of ATP13A4 ATPase activity using ADP-Glo Max Assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"82juh","text":"This protocol describes a luminescence-based ADP-Glo Max assay (Promega) to measure the ATPase activity of ATP13A4 by detecting the rate of ATP hydrolysis. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"f50se","text":"Allow ADP-Glo Max Assay components to reach room temperature before use!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"1vpvo","text":"ADP-Glo Max Assay (Cat# V7002; Promega)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5idc","text":"Purified ATP13A4 (100 ng per reaction)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71oa8","text":"Putrescine (Cat# P7505; Sigma-Aldrich)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4c214","text":"Spermidine (Cat# S2626; Sigma-Aldrich)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"esck2","text":"Spermine (Cat# 85590; Sigma-Aldrich)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2jg3u","text":"MgCl₂ hexahydrate (Cat# M2670; Sigma-Aldrich)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8egbr","text":"KCl (Cat# P9541; Sigma-Aldrich)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"39bno","text":"MOPS (Cat# A1076.1000; VWR)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3s49d","text":"KOH (Cat# 221473; Sigma-Aldrich)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2a21q","text":"Dithiothreitol (DTT; Cat# A2948.0025; VWR)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bqb1b","text":"n-Dodecyl-β-D-maltoside (DDM; Cat# 1758-1350; Inalco)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7jgs3","text":"Synergy H1 microplate reader (Biotek)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nfpf","text":"GraphPad Prism Software","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare a 25 µl reaction mixture containing: 100 ng purified ATP13A4, 11 mM MgCl 2 , 100 mM KCl, 50 mM MOPS-KOH (pH 7), 1 mM DTT, 0,001% (wt/vol) DDM, and desired polyamine concentrations.
Add ATP to a final concentration of 5 millimolar (mM) to initiate the reaction.
Following this, incubate the reaction for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Add 25 µL of ADP-Glo reagent to each reaction and incubate for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .

Add 50 µL of ADP-Glo Max Detection Reagent to each reaction and incubate for 01:00:00 in the dark at Room temperature . Protect samples from light during incubation with ADP-Glo Max Detection Reagent.
Measure luminescence using a compatible microplate reader ( e.g. Synergy H1 microplate reader; Biotek).
Analyze the luminescence data using GraphPad Prism Software. Perform curve fitting to evaluate ATPase activity.
creator: Sarah van Veen
